006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.util;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.FileUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
023
024import java.util.*;
025import java.util.regex.*;
026
027import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
028
029/**
030 * A parsed path pattern constructed from a {@link RestOp#path() @RestOp(path)} value.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Handles aspects of matching and precedence ordering.
034 *
035 */
036public abstract sealed class UrlPathMatcher implements Comparable<UrlPathMatcher> permits UrlPathMatcher.FileNameMatcher, UrlPathMatcher.PathMatcher {
037
038   /**
039    * A file name pattern such as "favicon.ico" or "*.jsp".
040    */
041   @SuppressWarnings({
042      "java:S2160" // equals() is inherited from UrlPathMatcher which compares by pattern string; added fields are derived from the pattern
043   })
044   static final class FileNameMatcher extends UrlPathMatcher {
045
046      private final String basePattern;
047      private final String extPattern;
048      private final String comparator;
049
050      FileNameMatcher(String pattern) {
051         super(pattern);
052         var base = getBaseName(pattern);
053         var ext = getFileExtension(pattern);
054         basePattern = base.equals("*") ? null : base;
055         extPattern = ext.equals("*") ? null : ext;
056         this.comparator = pattern.replaceAll("\\w+", "X").replace("*", "W");
057      }
058
059      @Override /* Overridden from UrlPathMatcher */
060      public String getComparator() { return comparator; }
061
062      @Override /* Overridden from UrlPathMatcher */
063      public UrlPathMatch match(UrlPath pathInfo) {
064         Optional<String> fileName = pathInfo.getFileName();
065         if (fileName.isPresent()) {
066            var base = getBaseName(fileName.get());
067            var ext = getFileExtension(fileName.get());
068            if ((basePattern == null || basePattern.equals(base)) && (extPattern == null || extPattern.equals(ext)))
069               return new UrlPathMatch(pathInfo.getPath(), pathInfo.getParts().length, new String[0], new String[0]);
070         }
071         return null;
072      }
073   }
074
075   /**
076    * A dir name pattern such as "/foo" or "/*".
077    */
078   @SuppressWarnings({
079      "java:S2160" // equals() is inherited from UrlPathMatcher which compares by pattern string; added fields are derived from the pattern
080   })
081   static final class PathMatcher extends UrlPathMatcher {
082      private static final Pattern VAR_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("\\{([^\\}]+)\\}");
083
084      private final String pattern;
085      private final String comparator;
086      private final String[] parts;
087      private final String[] vars;
088      private final String[] varKeys;
089      private final boolean hasRemainder;
090
091      PathMatcher(String patternString) {
092         super(patternString);
093         if (ie(patternString)) {
094            this.pattern = "/";
095         } else if (patternString.charAt(0) != '/') {
096            this.pattern = '/' + patternString;
097         } else {
098            this.pattern = patternString;
099         }
100
101         var c = patternString.replaceAll("\\{[^\\}]+\\}", ".").replaceAll("\\w+", "X").replace(".", "W");
102         if (c.isEmpty())
103            c = "+";
104         if (! c.endsWith("/*"))
105            c = c + "/W";
106         this.comparator = c;
107
108         String[] parts2 = new UrlPath(pattern).getParts();
109
110         this.hasRemainder = parts2.length > 0 && "*".equals(parts2[parts2.length - 1]);
111
112         parts2 = hasRemainder ? Arrays.copyOf(parts2, parts2.length - 1) : parts2;
113
114         this.parts = parts2;
115         this.vars = new String[parts2.length];
116         List<String> vars2 = list();
117
118         for (var i = 0; i < parts2.length; i++) {
119            Matcher m = VAR_PATTERN.matcher(parts2[i]);
120            if (m.matches()) {
121               this.vars[i] = m.group(1);
122               vars2.add(this.vars[i]);
123            }
124         }
125
126         this.varKeys = vars2.isEmpty() ? null : vars2.toArray(new String[vars2.size()]);
127      }
128
129      @Override
130      public String getComparator() { return comparator; }
131
132      @Override
133      public String[] getVars() { return varKeys == null ? new String[0] : Arrays.copyOf(varKeys, varKeys.length); }
134
135      @Override
136      public boolean hasVars() {
137         return nn(varKeys);
138      }
139
140      /**
141       * Returns a non-<jk>null</jk> value if the specified path matches this pattern.
142       *
143       * @param urlPath The path to match against.
144       * @return
145       *    A pattern match object, or <jk>null</jk> if the path didn't match this pattern.
146       */
147      @SuppressWarnings({
148         "null", // Null handling verified by context or framework
149         "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
150      })
151      @Override
152      public UrlPathMatch match(UrlPath urlPath) {
153
154         String[] pip = urlPath.getParts();
155
156         if (parts.length != pip.length) {
157            if (hasRemainder) {
158               if ((pip.length == parts.length - 1 && ! urlPath.isTrailingSlash()) || pip.length < parts.length)
159                  return null;
160            } else {
161               if (pip.length != parts.length + 1 || ! urlPath.isTrailingSlash())
162                  return null;
163            }
164         }
165
166         for (var i = 0; i < parts.length; i++)
167            if (vars[i] == null && (pip.length <= i || ! ("*".equals(parts[i]) || pip[i].equals(parts[i]))))
168               return null;
169
170         String[] vals = varKeys == null ? null : new String[varKeys.length];
171
172         var j = 0;
173         if (nn(vals))
174            for (var i = 0; i < parts.length; i++)
175               if (nn(vars[i]))
176                  vals[j++] = pip[i];
177
178         return new UrlPathMatch(urlPath.getPath(), parts.length, varKeys, vals);
179      }
180   }
181
182   /**
183    * Constructs a matcher from the specified pattern string.
184    *
185    * @param pattern The pattern string.
186    * @return A new matcher.
187    */
188   public static UrlPathMatcher of(String pattern) {
189      pattern = emptyIfNull(pattern);
190      boolean isFilePattern = pattern.matches("[^\\/\\.]+\\.[^\\/]+");
191      return isFilePattern ? new FileNameMatcher(pattern) : new PathMatcher(pattern);
192
193   }
194
195   private final String pattern;
196
197   UrlPathMatcher(String pattern) {
198      this.pattern = pattern;
199   }
200
201   /**
202    * Comparator for this object.
203    *
204    * <p>
205    * The comparator is designed to order URL pattern from most-specific to least-specific.
206    * For example, the following patterns would be ordered as follows:
207    * <ol>
208    *    <li><c>foo.bar</c>
209    *    <li><c>*.bar</c>
210    *    <li><c>/foo/bar</c>
211    *    <li><c>/foo/bar/*</c>
212    *    <li><c>/foo/{id}/bar</c>
213    *    <li><c>/foo/{id}/bar/*</c>
214    *    <li><c>/foo/{id}</c>
215    *    <li><c>/foo/{id}/*</c>
216    *    <li><c>/foo</c>
217    *    <li><c>/foo/*</c>
218    * </ol>
219    */
220   @Override /* Overridden from Comparable */
221   public int compareTo(UrlPathMatcher o) {
222      return o.getComparator().compareTo(getComparator());
223   }
224
225   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
226   public boolean equals(Object o) {
227      return o instanceof UrlPathMatcher other && compareTo(other) == 0;
228   }
229
230   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
231   public int hashCode() {
232      return getComparator() != null ? getComparator().hashCode() : 0;
233   }
234
235   /**
236    * Returns the variable names found in the pattern.
237    *
238    * @return
239    *    The variable names or an empty array if no variables found.
240    * <br>Modifying the returned array does not modify this object.
241    */
242   public String[] getVars() { return new String[0]; }
243
244   /**
245    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this path pattern contains variables.
246    *
247    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this path pattern contains variables.
248    */
249   public boolean hasVars() {
250      return false;
251   }
252
253   /**
254    * Returns a non-<jk>null</jk> value if the specified path matches this pattern.
255    *
256    * @param pathInfo The path to match against.
257    * @return
258    *    A pattern match object, or <jk>null</jk> if the path didn't match this pattern.
259    */
260   public abstract UrlPathMatch match(UrlPath pathInfo);
261
262   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
263   public String toString() {
264      return pattern;
265   }
266
267   /**
268    * Returns a string that can be used to compare this matcher with other matchers to provide the ability to
269    * order URL patterns from most-specific to least-specific.
270    *
271    * @return A comparison string.
272    */
273   protected abstract String getComparator();
274}
